_INSTRUCTIONS_FOR_PLAYER: === LEVEL-UP HELPER ===
Use this when your character levels up and you want AI help recalculating everything.
1. Open your character sheet in the app and copy your current stats
(or export from Dev tools if available)
2. Open Gemini or any AI chat
3. Paste this template AND your current character data
4. Tell the AI: 'Level up this character from level N to level N+1.
Here is their current sheet: [paste current data]'
5. For subclass choices, feats, ASIs, or spell swaps — tell the AI what you want:
'Take the Battle Master subclass, choose Riposte and Trip Attack maneuvers'
'Take the Tough feat instead of ASI'
'Swap Sleep for Misty Step, prepare Shatter and Hold Person'
6. Copy the AI's output
7. In the app: open character sheet > Edit > '📋 Update from JSON'
8. KEEP 'Preserve HP, XP, conditions & inventory' CHECKED
(your HP/XP/gear stay, everything else updates)
9. Tap Apply
TIPS:
- You can level up multiple levels at once: 'Level from 3 to 5'
- Ask the AI to explain what's new: 'What do I get at Rogue 5?'
- For multiclass dips: 'Level up to Fighter 4 / Warlock 1, I'm dipping Warlock'
- The app's built-in level-up wizard also handles this — this template is for when
you want more control or AI help choosing options
- To add your class to the built-in level-up wizard, use class-progression-template.json
(☰ menu > Import Class Progression). Then the wizard handles everything automatically.
_INSTRUCTIONS_FOR_AI: INSTRUCTIONS FOR THE AI:
The player is leveling up their D&D 5e character. They will paste their current character data below.
Recalculate everything for the new level. Keep all existing data intact except what changes on level-up.
WHAT CHANGES ON LEVEL-UP:
- hp_max: add hit die average + CON mod (d6→4, d8→5, d10→6, d12→7, plus CON modifier)
- Proficiency bonus: increases at levels 5, 9, 13, 17 — recalculate ALL skills, saves, spell DCs, attack bonuses
- Class features: add new features for the new level
- Subclass: chosen at level 3 (most classes) — add subclass features
- ASI/Feat: at levels 4, 8, 12, 16, 19 — apply stat increases or feat
- Spell slots: update slots array with new max values
- Spells known/prepared: add new spells, allow swaps if the class supports it
- Cantrips: some classes gain cantrips at certain levels
- Resources: update max values (e.g. Bardic Inspiration uses = CHA mod, Rage uses increase)
- Extra Attack, other scaling features
WHAT STAYS THE SAME:
- name, race, background, alignment, color, id
- ability scores (unless ASI is applied)
- inventory, conditions, xp, backstory fields
- languages (unless a feat adds one)
FORMAT RULES:
- Stats: '16 (+3)' format with modifier in parens
- features: add new features to existing list (don't remove old ones)
- skills: recalculate modifiers if proficiency bonus changed
- Update the 'level' field
- Update the 'class' field if subclass was chosen: 'Fighter (Battle Master)'
OUTPUT: Return ONLY the complete character JSON object. No wrapper, no code fences, no commentary.
The player will paste it into a single-character import dialog with 'Preserve HP/XP' checked.
_PASTE_CURRENT_CHARACTER_BELOW: Copy your character's current JSON here when talking to the AI. The AI needs your current data to level you up correctly.
